name: IT-arch-gc1-multiline-guard
description: The GC1 internal-jest-mock guard (eslint-rules/no-internal-jest-mock.mjs and/or scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts) detects single-line internal jest.mock('./...') / jest.mock('../...') / jest.mock('@eduagent/...') calls but misses calls written across multiple lines (the specifier on a separate physical line from `jest.mock(`). This is a guard-bypass blind spot (F-156): a contributor can add an internal mock in multiline form and slip it past CI. Fix the guard so multiline internal jest.mock calls are detected.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: the guard matches `jest.mock` + specifier on one physical line; a multiline `jest.mock(\n  './x',\n  () => ...)` is not matched. Done (outcome): the guard flags internal jest.mock calls regardless of line breaks between `jest.mock(` and the specifier, while still ignoring external bare-specifier mocks (no false positives). Regression test: a fixture containing a MULTILINE internal jest.mock is reported RED by the guard — demonstrated failing against the pre-fix guard (revert/restore) and passing after; existing single-line detection and external-mock pass-through stay green. Re-grep the actual guard implementation first (eslint-rules/no-internal-jest-mock.mjs + scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts) to confirm which layer needs the fix.
